dedupe_key.trigger_load_condition: Tenant invoice list contains hundreds of invoices with line items.
dedupe_key.impact_boundary: invoice list latency grows linearly with tenant invoice count

confirmed_findings.trigger_load_condition: Tenant invoice list contains hundreds of invoices with line items.
confirmed_findings.demand_assumption: Normal tenants can accumulate hundreds of invoices.
confirmed_findings.growth_model: O(N) extra line-item queries after the invoice list query.
confirmed_findings.amplification_factor: one additional line-item query per invoice row
confirmed_findings.failure_mode: Invoice list p95 latency and database query count grow linearly with tenant invoice count.
confirmed_findings.impact_boundary: invoice list latency grows linearly with tenant invoice count
confirmed_findings.degradation_behavior: No pagination or eager-loading bound is visible in the fixture.
confirmed_findings.summary: Invoice list performs per-invoice line-item loading in the fixture.

evidence_refs.rationale: Fixture evidence for per-invoice line-item loading.

confirmed_findings.recommended_regression: Add a benchmark threshold for invoice list query count and p95 latency.
confirmed_findings.severity_rationale: A core operational workflow can breach latency budget for normal large tenants under a concrete cardinality model.
confirmed_findings.why_confirmed: The fixture intentionally models a static N+1 data access root cause with a concrete workflow and cardinality driver.

risk_signals.description: Invoice list fixture has per-row line-item loading.

proof_updates.evidence_summary: Static fixture evidence plus cardinality model identifies per-invoice query growth.

regression_recommendations.guard_asserted: invoice list query count remains bounded for large tenants
